graph.topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
graph.researcher_bg: An HCI researcher interested in spatial computing and human creativity. He wants to understand the cognitive load and daily friction animators face when translating abstract ideas into 3D movements. He views animation primarily through the lens of spatial trajectories, timing data, and interaction efficiency, but lacks firsthand experience in the grueling, frame-by-frame artistic production process.
graph.expert_bg: A Senior 3D Animator and VFX Supervisor in the film industry. She manages complex animation workflows and relies heavily on physical intuition, acting out scenes, and a deep understanding of weight and timing to execute exact directorial visions.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Decoding Directorial Intent
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: The challenge of translating abstract emotional direction into concrete mechanical changes during the briefings and note sessions.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Translating Abstract Notes
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: Interpreting vague feedback like 'make it pop' into specific animation curves and timing adjustments.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Decoding 'Energy' vs. 'Spacing'
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: They'll tell you 'make it pop' or 'add more energy' when they actually mean 'slow down the spacing before the hit'. You have to hear the technical fix behind the emotional note.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The Evolving Brief
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The brief isn't a static document; it changes once they see the block-out. You can't standardize the input because the goalpost moves during the first pass.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Emotional Rhythm
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Identifying the subtle timing beats that convey character internal state rather than just physical movement.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Finding the Silence
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: It's not about the movement; it's about the pause before the movement. You have to feel where the character is thinking, and how they interact with the environment, before you set a single keyframe.

q_01__exp_L1_01.label: Embodied Animation Process
q_01__exp_L1_01.description: The physical and kinesthetic methods animators use to bridge the gap between their own body and the digital rig.

q_01__exp_L2_01_00.label: Physical Reference Gathering
q_01__exp_L2_01_00.description: Methods for capturing realistic motion data, ranging from video lookup to personal acting.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Acting It Out Yourself
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Video reference is okay, but you gotta get up and act it out in the mirror yourself to really feel the weight transfer. That's where the truth is.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Kinesthetic Weight Transfer
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: You gotta feel the gravity of the character in your own shoulders before you touch the spline. If you don't feel heavy, the rig won't look heavy.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: Internal & Paper Sketching
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: Before touching the rig, I sketch in my head or on paper. It's about getting the feel right and capturing the essence before you even start animating digitally.

q_01__exp_L2_01_01.label: Block-out Validation
q_01__exp_L2_01_01.description: Techniques used to verify the clarity and readability of the initial pose-to-pose animation.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Silhouette Readability Check
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: I squint at the screen until everything blurs. If I can't tell what they're doing just from the black shape, the pose is wrong, no matter how good the curves are.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Internal Feeling Validation
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: We don't standardize much because every character has its own soul. If you force a template, you lose it. It's about staying in the flow and ensuring it feels right internally.

q_01__exp_L1_02.label: Workflow & Standardization Friction
q_01__exp_L1_02.description: The tension between rigid pipeline efficiency requirements and the non-linear reality of creative exploration.

q_01__exp_L2_02_00.label: Process Modeling
q_01__exp_L2_02_00.description: Critiques of how external observers or tools model the animation creation process.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Linear HCI Model Mismatch
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: HCI thinks it's A-to-B, brief-to-block. But it's actually A-to-C-to-A-to-B. You can't streamline the messiness out of creativity without killing the performance.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: Tool Intuition Friction
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: You have a clear image in your head, but the software fights you. It's a constant loop between what you imagine and what the system actually lets you do.

q_01__exp_L2_02_01.label: Team Efficiency
q_01__exp_L2_02_01.description: Balancing the need for standardized files and naming conventions with the need for individual artistic expression.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: Over-Standardization Kills Vibe
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: You can standardize the file naming, sure. But if you try to standardize how we find the acting choice, you just get robotic motion across the whole team.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_01.label: Speed vs. Fidelity Balance
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_01.description: If you rush the block-out, you redo it later. If you linger, you lose momentum. Efficiency is knowing when to push through and when to step back and re-act.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.

q_01__res_L1_00.label: Workflow Topology & Linearity
q_01__res_L1_00.description: Examining the actual structure of the production pipeline versus the assumed linear model, focusing on iteration loops and information entry points.

q_01__res_L2_00_00.label: Iteration Dynamics
q_01__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding the frequency and triggers for looping back to earlier stages during the block-out phase.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Brief Revisitation Frequency
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: How often do you return to the director's brief or reference materials after you have already started manipulating the 3D rig?

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Technical Phase Exit Criteria
q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: What specific technical markers (e.g., keyframes set, constraints applied) signal that the block-out stage is complete, distinct from artistic approval?

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Information Modality
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Analyzing the format and medium of the initial instructions provided to the animator.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Brief Media Format
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Is the director's brief primarily delivered as text, video reference, verbal discussion, or a combination, and how does this affect initial setup time?

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Interaction Efficiency & Cognitive Load
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Investigating the friction between the animator's mental model and the software interface during the creation of spatial trajectories.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Interface & Workspace
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: How the physical and digital workspace is arranged to support the translation of ideas into 3D space.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Screen Real Estate Allocation
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: How do you arrange reference images or video plates relative to the 3D viewport to minimize eye movement and attention splitting?

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Asset Loading Latency
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Does the time required to load rigs or scene files interrupt your creative flow state during early experimentation?

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: System Performance & Friction
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Identifying manual tasks that introduce unnecessary cognitive load or interaction cost.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Manual Keyframing Overhead
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Which repetitive spatial adjustments in the block-out phase lack tool automation and require manual keyframe insertion?

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Standardization & Pipeline Governance
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Exploring the protocols used to maintain team efficiency and data integrity without stifling individual creative exploration.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Versioning & Handoff
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Methods for tracking progress and preparing data for downstream stages in the production pipeline.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: File Versioning Conventions
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: What naming conventions or save structures do you use to track iterations during the block-out phase to prevent data loss or confusion?

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Downstream Data Structure
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: What specific data layers or file formats are packaged and passed to the next stage (e.g., lighting, rigging) after block-out?

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Team Consistency Protocols
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Rules enforced to ensure technical consistency across different animators working on the same project.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Standardized Rig Usage
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: How do you ensure different animators manipulate the same character rig controls consistently to avoid technical conflicts later?

assistance_payload.validate_focus: The expert emphasizes the nuance of decoding cryptic director notes, such as translating 'energy' into specific spacing adjustments. This ability to hear the technical fix behind emotional language is vital for execution.

pivot.limitation: Staying only on this decoding process misses how the expert anchors their work against the static requirements before dynamic notes arrive.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher seeks to understand the frequency with which the animator returns to the original brief during rig manipulation.
pivot.compelling_reason: Both the dynamic notes and the static brief shape the workflow, so ignoring brief revisitation obscures where intent drift originally begins.
pivot.coarse_scenario: While decoding those live notes is crucial, I also need to understand how often you check the original brief while working, because that tells us where your initial anchor point lies versus the new direction.

structural_mapping.inputs: Subjective complaint ('Too tight' / 'Needs energy')
structural_mapping.logic: Interpret feeling as specific physical change (Let out seam / Slow down spacing)
structural_mapping.outputs: Physical state that satisfies the feeling (Garment fits / Animation pops)

analogy.explanation: Your concept separates technical state from artistic intent. But like a tailor knowing 'fits right' defines 'sewing done,' the expert sees the artistic note as the specific technical instruction required to close the phase. The mechanism is identical: subjective feedback dictates objective completion.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you hear 'add energy,' do you treat the spacing fix as a separate technical task you finish before approval, or is solving that note the only way to actually finish the technical phase?

pivot.coarse_scenario: I agree that acting it out is essential for quality, but I need to understand if the brief format affects your setup time, because that determines how much time you actually have for that acting process.

q_01__shared_root.label: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Decoding Directorial Intent
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: The challenge of translating abstract emotional direction into concrete mechanical changes during the briefings and note sessions.

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Translating Abstract Notes
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: Interpreting vague feedback like 'make it pop' into specific animation curves and timing adjustments.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Decoding 'Energy' vs. 'Spacing'
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: They'll tell you 'make it pop' or 'add more energy' when they actually mean 'slow down the spacing before the hit'. You have to hear the technical fix behind the emotional note.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The Evolving Brief
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The brief isn't a static document; it changes once they see the block-out. You can't standardize the input because the goalpost moves during the first pass.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Emotional Rhythm
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Identifying the subtle timing beats that convey character internal state rather than just physical movement.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Finding the Silence
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: It's not about the movement; it's about the pause before the movement. You have to feel where the character is thinking, and how they interact with the environment, before you set a single keyframe.

q_01__exp_L1_01.label: Embodied Animation Process
q_01__exp_L1_01.description: The physical and kinesthetic methods animators use to bridge the gap between their own body and the digital rig.

q_01__exp_L2_01_00.label: Physical Reference Gathering
q_01__exp_L2_01_00.description: Methods for capturing realistic motion data, ranging from video lookup to personal acting.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Acting It Out Yourself
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Video reference is okay, but you gotta get up and act it out in the mirror yourself to really feel the weight transfer. That's where the truth is.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Kinesthetic Weight Transfer
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: You gotta feel the gravity of the character in your own shoulders before you touch the spline. If you don't feel heavy, the rig won't look heavy.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: Internal & Paper Sketching
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: Before touching the rig, I sketch in my head or on paper. It's about getting the feel right and capturing the essence before you even start animating digitally.

q_01__exp_L2_01_01.label: Block-out Validation
q_01__exp_L2_01_01.description: Techniques used to verify the clarity and readability of the initial pose-to-pose animation.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Silhouette Readability Check
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: I squint at the screen until everything blurs. If I can't tell what they're doing just from the black shape, the pose is wrong, no matter how good the curves are.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Internal Feeling Validation
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: We don't standardize much because every character has its own soul. If you force a template, you lose it. It's about staying in the flow and ensuring it feels right internally.

q_01__exp_L1_02.label: Workflow & Standardization Friction
q_01__exp_L1_02.description: The tension between rigid pipeline efficiency requirements and the non-linear reality of creative exploration.

q_01__exp_L2_02_00.label: Process Modeling
q_01__exp_L2_02_00.description: Critiques of how external observers or tools model the animation creation process.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Linear HCI Model Mismatch
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: HCI thinks it's A-to-B, brief-to-block. But it's actually A-to-C-to-A-to-B. You can't streamline the messiness out of creativity without killing the performance.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: Tool Intuition Friction
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: You have a clear image in your head, but the software fights you. It's a constant loop between what you imagine and what the system actually lets you do.

q_01__exp_L2_02_01.label: Team Efficiency
q_01__exp_L2_02_01.description: Balancing the need for standardized files and naming conventions with the need for individual artistic expression.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: Over-Standardization Kills Vibe
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: You can standardize the file naming, sure. But if you try to standardize how we find the acting choice, you just get robotic motion across the whole team.

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_01.label: Speed vs. Fidelity Balance
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_01.description: If you rush the block-out, you redo it later. If you linger, you lose momentum. Efficiency is knowing when to push through and when to step back and re-act.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Interaction Efficiency & Cognitive Load

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Interface & Workspace
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: How the physical and digital workspace is arranged to support the translation of ideas into 3D space.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Asset Loading Latency

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: System Performance & Friction

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Manual Keyframing Overhead
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Which repetitive spatial adjustments in the block-out phase lack tool automation and require manual keyframe insertion?

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Standardization & Pipeline Governance
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Exploring the protocols used to maintain team efficiency and data integrity without stifling individual creative exploration.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: Versioning & Handoff
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Methods for tracking progress and preparing data for downstream stages in the production pipeline.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: File Versioning Conventions
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: What naming conventions or save structures do you use to track iterations during the block-out phase to prevent data loss or confusion?

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Downstream Data Structure
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: What specific data layers or file formats are packaged and passed to the next stage (e.g., lighting, rigging) after block-out?

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Team Consistency Protocols
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Rules enforced to ensure technical consistency across different animators working on the same project.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Standardized Rig Usage
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: How do you ensure different animators manipulate the same character rig controls consistently to avoid technical conflicts later?

q_02__exp_L1_00.label: Director-Animator Communication Gap
q_02__exp_L1_00.description: The friction caused by differing vocabularies and expectations between creative leadership and technical execution.

q_02__exp_L2_00_00.label: Vocabulary Mismatch
q_02__exp_L2_00_00.description: The difficulty in translating artistic direction into technical parameters.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Directors don't speak graph editor
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: If I start talking about spline tangents or bezier handles, I lose them immediately. Coordinates are just scaffolding; we agree on the feel first, numbers come later.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Emotional adjectives vs. Physical values
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: They'll say 'make it sadder,' and I have to figure out if that means slower timing, lower posture, or less eye movement. It's a decoding process every time.

q_02__exp_L2_00_01.label: Documentation Medium
q_02__exp_L2_00_01.description: Choosing the right format to capture intent without losing nuance.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Video reference & Live Demo
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: I'd rather shoot myself on an iPhone or act it out in the room with the director than write a paragraph. A quick blocking pass in software cuts through confusion faster than emails.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Rough Rhythm vs Exact Frames
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: I might sketch a rough timing chart to show the rhythm, but never exact frames. If I stick to the spreadsheet numbers, the joke dies because the pause was too short.

q_02__exp_L1_01.label: Physical Intuition & Workflow
q_02__exp_L1_01.description: Reliance on embodied knowledge and physical sensing to drive digital creation.

q_02__exp_L2_01_00.label: Embodied Knowledge
q_02__exp_L2_01_00.description: Using the animator's own body to understand the character's mechanics.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Acting it out yourself
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: You gotta feel the breath before the move. If I don't physically act it out in the room, the digital character feels hollow no matter how good the curves look.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Sensing the center of gravity
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Knowing when a heavy object feels light because of the follow-through. It's not about the mass value, it's about how the weight shifts during the turn.

q_02__exp_L2_01_01.label: Technical Translation
q_02__exp_L2_01_01.description: The limitations of software tools when representing organic movement.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Smooth curves don't mean good acting
q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: Perfect bezier curves look robotic. Real movement is messy, so I often have to break the software's smoothing tools to get something that feels human.

q_02__exp_L1_02.label: Preserving Artistic Intent
q_02__exp_L1_02.description: Challenges in maintaining the original creative vision through technical iteration.

q_02__exp_L2_02_00.label: Iteration Risk
q_02__exp_L2_02_00.description: The danger of losing the spark or flow during revision cycles.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Fixing one frame breaks the flow
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: You fix the hand and the shoulder dies. It's like pulling a thread on a sweater; you tweak one keyframe for the director and the whole kinetic chain feels off.

q_02__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.

q_02__res_L1_00.label: Semantic Translation of Intent
q_02__res_L1_00.description: Investigating how abstract creative directives are mentally converted into concrete spatial and temporal data by the animator.

q_02__res_L2_00_00.label: Vocabulary & Concept Mapping
q_02__res_L2_00_00.description: Analyzing the linguistic and conceptual bridge between director language and animation data.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Mapping adjectives to spatial curves
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Investigate how qualitative adjectives (e.g., 'heavy', 'snappy') are mentally mapped to quantitative spline curves or motion paths.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Timing communication units
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Determine if timing is negotiated in absolute units (frames) or relative units (beats) during the intent definition phase.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_02.label: Terminology friction points
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_02.description: Identify specific instances where film direction terminology conflicts with technical animation constraints during discussion.

q_02__res_L2_00_01.label: Cognitive Load of Interpretation
q_02__res_L2_00_01.description: Understanding the mental effort required to decode vague directives into actionable production tasks.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Mental effort in decoding directives
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Measure the cognitive load associated with translating vague verbal directives into precise spatial data without visual aids.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Verification of shared understanding
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Explore mechanisms used to verify shared mental models between director and animator before production begins.

q_02__res_L1_01.label: Collaborative Artifacts & Tools
q_02__res_L1_01.description: Examining the physical and digital mediums used to record, share, and annotate movement intent during collaboration.

q_02__res_L2_01_00.label: Documentation Modalities
q_02__res_L2_01_00.description: Cataloging the forms of records that capture dynamic intent outside of the animation software itself.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Formal vs informal records
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Catalog whether intent is captured in persistent artifacts (notes, diagrams) or remains as ephemeral verbal agreements.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Video reference as specification
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Assess if video reference serves as a binding spatial specification or merely inspirational guidance during intent setting.

q_02__res_L2_01_01.label: Spatial Interaction Needs
q_02__res_L2_01_01.description: Identifying gaps in current tools for expressing and annotating 3D movement trajectories during review.

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: 3D annotation capabilities
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Identify gaps in current review tools for annotating 3D trajectories directly within the viewport during feedback sessions.

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Spatial computing for trajectories
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Evaluate interest in spatial computing interfaces (VR/AR) for demonstrating motion paths physically rather than verbally.

q_03__exp_L1_00.label: Evaluation Heuristics Beyond the Graph
q_03__exp_L1_00.description: The specific visual, physical, and somatic cues used to validate weight and rhythm that cannot be quantified by software metrics.

q_03__exp_L2_00_00.label: Visual Stability Cues
q_03__exp_L2_00_00.description: Checking for subtle visual errors that break the illusion of mass and gravity during static or slow moments.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Ground Contact Slip
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: If the foot slides even a pixel during a weight shift, the illusion of mass collapses. It's not about the model, it's about the anchor.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Micro-Movement in Holds
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: A heavy character isn't still; they're breathing against the gravity. If the hold is too clean, they look like a statue, not a tired worker.

q_03__exp_L2_00_01.label: Kinetic Chain Integrity
q_03__exp_L2_00_01.description: Verifying that the transfer of energy through the body parts matches the expected physical properties of the character.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Drag Timing vs. Material
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Loose flesh lags differently than tight muscle. You can't measure it in frames, you have to feel the resistance in your own arm.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Overlap Consistency
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: If the hair overlaps left but the coat overlaps right, the brain rejects the physics immediately. Directionality must match the force.

q_03__exp_L2_00_02.label: Embodied Validation
q_03__exp_L2_00_02.description: Using the animator's own physiological responses as a primary metric for evaluating digital movement.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Somatic Mirror Response
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: If the movement doesn't make my shoulders tense up the way it should, something's off. I feel the weight in my own body while watching.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Holistic Weight Illusion
q_03__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: The only test that counts is when I watch it and can't tell if it's real or not. If the character feels like they're moving through water, the numbers don't matter.

q_03__exp_L1_01.label: The Formalization Gap
q_03__exp_L1_01.description: The inherent difficulties in documenting, teaching, or replicating subjective animation judgments across a team.

q_03__exp_L2_01_00.label: Mentorship & Feedback Loops
q_03__exp_L2_01_00.description: The reliance on shared experience and osmosis rather than written documentation to convey quality standards.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Subjective Note Translation
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Telling a junior 'make it heavier' is useless unless they've felt what you feel. Writing it down as a formula kills the soul. Transfer happens when we act it out together in the room.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Tool Reliance vs. Visual Trust
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Juniors look at the graph; veterans look at the screen. Numbers are just scaffolding or placeholders. Tools lie about weight, so you re-act until it clicks.

q_03__exp_L2_01_01.label: Contextual Rule Breaking
q_03__exp_L2_01_01.description: Situations where established physical principles must be intentionally violated to serve narrative or stylistic goals.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Narrative Physics Overrides
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: Sometimes a heavy punch needs to look light for comedy. Physics says one thing, the joke says another.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Stylization Specifics
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: Cartoon rigs don't follow real-world inertia. You need a different rulebook for squash-and-stretch than for realistic IK.

q_03__res_L2_00_00.description: Exploring whether objective metrics exist beneath subjective descriptions of weight and rhythm.

q_03__res_L2_00_01.description: Understanding the decision-making process that signals when a rhythm is 'correct'.

q_03__res_L1_01.label: Formalization for Transfer

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Parameterizing style vs. soul
q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Assess which aspects of rhythm can be standardized into sliders or parameters without resulting in robotic, soulless motion.

q_03__res_L2_01_01.label: Tool Mediation
q_03__res_L2_01_01.description: Evaluating the potential for software to assist in formalizing intuition.

q_03__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Gauge animator willingness to accept AI-suggested timing adjustments based on physical weight calculations versus manual keying.

q_03__res_L2_02_00.label: Interaction Costs
q_03__res_L2_02_00.description: Measuring the friction caused by switching between visual and data representations during evaluation.

q_03__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Measure mental simulation fatigue
q_03__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Investigate how prolonged reliance on internal kinesthetic sensing impacts animator fatigue and error detection rates over a session.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You hit all the contact points and the weight shift is accurate, but it looks like a puppet. Director calls it 'soulless' or 'empty'. The rig didn't lie, but the intention did.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Missing Internal Monologue
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The character moves before they think. Missing the 'want' or motivation (fear, urgency) that drives the physical action. Nobody's home behind the eyes.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Physiological Cues (Breath/Inertia)
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: Chest isn't heaving before the lift, or missing resistance/inertia in limbs. Feels like no gravity or presence. You feel the strain in your gut but the rig is static.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Timing & Hesitation
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Missing the pause or hold before the strike. Movement is too fluid/efficient, lacking the human hesitation. It's about the pause, not just the motion.

q_04__exp_L2_00_01.description: Methods used to identify the root cause of a rejected animation shot.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Translating 'Dead' Feedback
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Director says 'It's dead' or 'No heartbeat'. They mean the performance lacks life, not the rig is broken. You have to hear 'add life' not 'fix physics'.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Pinpointing the 'Uncanny' Moment
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Scrubbing through to find the specific frame where smoothness breaks the illusion. Your brain goes 'ugh' at a specific spot, like frame 45.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: The Mirror Re-Act
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Stand up and act it out. If you feel tension or resistance in your shoulder that isn't in the rig, that's the missing keyframe. Re-establish physical presence.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Video Reference Comparison
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: Pull up reference video side-by-side. Sometimes you realize you copied the motion but missed the hesitation or weight transfer.

q_04__exp_L2_00_02.label: Technical Remediation
q_04__exp_L2_00_02.description: Specific adjustments made in the software to restore organic feel to the motion.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Over-Cleaned Splines
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: Smoothed the curves too much. Real movement is jittery. Made it too efficient, like a machine. No overshoots or locking in.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Injecting Imperfection
q_04__exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: Adding a little noise or hold where it shouldn't be. Sometimes the mistake or hesitation is the truth. Breaking the perfect arc.

q_04__shared_root.label: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
q_04__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.

q_04__res_L1_00.label: The Semantic Gap in Motion Quality
q_04__res_L1_00.description: Investigating the specific attributes that constitute 'technically correct but artistically wrong' movement, focusing on the disconnect between physics simulation and emotional resonance.

q_04__res_L2_00_00.label: Attributes of Rejected Perfection
q_04__res_L2_00_00.description: Identifying the specific mechanical elements that were flawless yet contributed to the artistic failure.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Primary vs. Secondary Motion Disconnect
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Determine if the rejection stemmed from perfect primary trajectories lacking necessary secondary overlap or follow-through nuance.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Timing Precision vs. Emotional Pacing
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Explore whether frame-perfect timing conflicted with the perceived psychological pacing required for the scene's mood.

q_04__res_L2_00_01.label: The Role of Intentional Imperfection
q_04__res_L2_00_01.description: Understanding if the 'invisible problem' was actually a lack of humanizing errors or noise in the data.

q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: The Uncanny Valley of Smoothness
q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Investigate if the movement was rejected because it was too mathematically smooth, lacking the micro-tremors of organic life.

q_04__res_L1_01.label: Diagnostic Heuristics for Subjective Failure
q_04__res_L1_01.description: Analyzing the cognitive strategies animators employ to troubleshoot errors that do not manifest as technical bugs or curve anomalies.

q_04__res_L2_01_00.label: Cognitive Debugging Processes
q_04__res_L2_01_00.description: Mapping the mental models used to isolate subjective errors when objective data appears correct.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Internal Simulation Comparison
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Uncover how the animator mentally simulated the intended weight versus the screen output to locate the dissonance.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Anatomical Isolation Tactics
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Identify if specific body parts (e.g., eyes, spine) were isolated mentally to pinpoint the source of the 'dead' feeling.

q_04__res_L2_01_01.label: Modal Switching for Diagnosis
q_04__res_L2_01_01.description: Examining changes in interaction mode required to reveal the invisible problem.

q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Kinesthetic Verification
q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Determine if physical acting was used specifically as a diagnostic tool to compare against the digital rig during this failure.

q_04__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: External Reference Overlay
q_04__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Assess if video reference was brought in post-failure to visually triangulate the missing quality.

q_04__res_L1_02.label: Tool & Interface Blind Spots
q_04__res_L1_02.description: Evaluating how the software interface and data visualization may have obscured the artistic error despite technical correctness.

q_04__res_L2_02_00.label: Visualization Limitations
q_04__res_L2_02_00.description: Identifying specific ways the UI masked the qualitative failure of the animation.

q_04__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Graph Editor Masking
q_04__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Determine if smooth spline curves in the Graph Editor hid the lack of tension or weight in the actual movement.

q_04__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Viewport Framing Issues
q_04__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Explore if the camera view or rig controls obscured the weight distribution that caused the director's rejection.

analogy.target_concept: Listening to an over-polished voice recording

structural_mapping.inputs: Raw audio waveforms corresponding to raw animation curves
structural_mapping.logic: Noise reduction filter corresponding to spline smoothing tool

analogy.explanation: The target shows the 'robotic voice' (Over-Cleaned Splines) is the visible output, while the 'expectation of breath' (Internal Simulation) is the hidden standard. Both concepts describe the same failure: the output lacks the noise signal the internal standard requires to feel real.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you smooth out the jitter to fix the curve, do you ever feel the weight is wrong because your brain expected a struggle the tool removed?

analogy.target_concept: Tracing over a signature until it looks printed

structural_mapping.inputs: Human-generated marks (animation curves / handwritten signature)
structural_mapping.logic: Automated smoothing for technical correctness (physics simulation / vector tracing)
structural_mapping.outputs: Sterile perfection that loses identity (machine-like motion / printed font)

analogy.explanation: Your attempt to fix 'disconnect' with physics acts like tracing the signature: it seeks technical correctness but erases the human hand the expert preserves through intentional noise.

assistance_payload.scenario: When a motion feels too robotic, do you ever add a stutter that violates physics, rather than just adding more secondary overlap to fix the disconnect?

structural_mapping.inputs: Flawless delivery or smooth curves
structural_mapping.logic: Removing variance removes humanity
structural_mapping.outputs: Audience distrusts sincerity or viewer sees machine

analogy.explanation: The researcher's 'Internal Simulation' seeks physical matches, but the expert's 'Over-Cleaned Splines' reveals that perfect matches feel fake. Like a rehearsed apology, if the delivery lacks stumbling blocks, it lacks sincerity. The researcher must realize the 'dissonance' they detect is often the intentional 'stutter' the expert needs for emotional truth.

assistance_payload.scenario: When you compare your mental simulation to the screen, do you ever keep a movement that breaks the laws of physics because it feels more honest than the accurate version?

main_bullets.label: Could you describe your standard workflow from receiving
main_bullets.source_question: 1. Could you describe your standard workflow from receiving a director's brief to producing the initial 3D block-out? In HCI, we often view this type of task as a linear information transfer process. How do you standardize these early stages to maximize your team's production efficiency?

main_bullets.label: When you discuss the 'dynamic intent' of a
main_bullets.source_question: 2. When you discuss the 'dynamic intent' of a character's movement with a director, how do you define and document it? Do you primarily communicate this intent using spatial coordinates, timing charts, or specific motion trajectories?

main_bullets.label: Animators often emphasize the importance of capturing the
main_bullets.source_question: 3. Animators often emphasize the importance of capturing the right 'weight' or 'rhythm' in a scene. When you evaluate whether a draft animation feels 'right', what objective metrics or explicit rules do you use? How do you formalize your physical intuition so that others can easily replicate it?

main_bullets.source_question: 4. Could you recall a specific project where the character's physical movement was technically flawless, but the director completely rejected it? What exactly was missing from that movement, and how did you diagnose that invisible problem?

_meta.topic: Understanding the traditional authoring workflows, physical-to-digital translation challenges, and intent expression bottlenecks of professional 3D character animators.
_meta.researcher_bg: An HCI researcher interested in spatial computing and human creativity. He wants to understand the cognitive load and daily friction animators face when translating abstract ideas into 3D movements. He views animation primarily through the lens of spatial trajectories, timing data, and interaction efficiency, but lacks firsthand experience in the grueling, frame-by-frame artistic production process.
_meta.expert_bg: A Senior 3D Animator and VFX Supervisor in the film industry. She manages complex animation workflows and relies heavily on physical intuition, acting out scenes, and a deep understanding of weight and timing to execute exact directorial visions.
